sites.name: Tunnel View
sites.blurb: The classic valley panorama: El Capitan, Bridalveil Fall, and Half Dome in one frame.

sites.name: Valley View
sites.blurb: Riverside view of El Capitan and Bridalveil Fall from the Merced's bank.

sites.name: Glacier Point
sites.blurb: 3,200 feet above the valley floor, facing Half Dome and the high country.

sites.name: Sentinel Bridge
sites.blurb: Half Dome reflected in the Merced — the classic evening shot.

sites.name: Taft Point
sites.blurb: Exposed granite ledge and fissures over the valley's south rim.

sites.name: Sentinel Dome
sites.blurb: 360° summit panorama a short walk off Glacier Point Road.

sites.name: Olmsted Point
sites.blurb: Glacial-polished granite looking down Tenaya Canyon toward Half Dome.

sites.name: Lower Yosemite Fall
sites.blurb: Base of North America's tallest waterfall; thunders in spring, fades by late summer.

sites.name: Bridalveil Fall
sites.blurb: 620-foot fall that flows year-round and swings in the wind.

sites.name: Happy Isles · Mist Trailhead
sites.blurb: Gateway to Vernal and Nevada Falls. Gage 11264500 reads the Merced here.

sites.name: Mirror Lake
sites.blurb: Seasonal lake under Half Dome's north face; best water in spring.

sites.name: El Capitan Meadow
sites.blurb: Straight-up view of the 3,000-foot wall; watch for climbers at dusk.

sites.name: Cook's Meadow
sites.blurb: Open meadow loop with Yosemite Falls, Half Dome, and frequent coyotes.

sites.name: Tuolumne Meadows
sites.blurb: The Sierra's largest subalpine meadow, along the Tuolumne River.

sites.name: Cathedral Lakes Trailhead
sites.blurb: High-country start toward Cathedral Peak and its granite-rimmed lakes.

sites.name: Tenaya Lake
sites.blurb: Granite-bound alpine lake right off Tioga Road.

sites.name: Mariposa Grove
sites.blurb: Five hundred giant sequoias, including the 3,000-year-old Grizzly Giant.

sites.name: O'Shaughnessy Dam · Hetch Hetchy
sites.blurb: Trail across the dam toward Wapama Falls; the park's quiet northwest.
